metadata.title: Why answering sub-questions in separate rooms makes an AI's reasoning more honest
metadata.topic: Isolated-context decomposition as a defense against motivated reasoning
metadata.purpose: Sealing each sub-answer in its own context removes access to the destination, so the sub-answerer has nothing to rationalize toward; the final answer is forced to be a function of independently computed pieces, not a post-hoc story.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: the headline decomposition-changes-faithfulness framing (top-level carded), recomposition-deference details, benchmark accuracy numbers
metadata.visual_object: A question routed into separate sealed rooms each returning one sub-answer, recomposed at the end — versus one open room where the answer leaks backward into the steps.
metadata.hook: Let a model reason in one pass and it can quietly bend the steps toward the answer it already wants; force each sub-question into a sealed context and it can't.

beats.narration_text: "Maiden name of the wife of the all-time NBA scoring leader" is split so a fresh model answers "Who is the wife of LeBron James?" with no sight of the original question or the answer choices — it cannot know which final option is convenient.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Chain-of-thought and factored decomposition ask the same sub-questions; why is the factored version's stated reasoning more faithful — actually determining its answer?
Let a model reason in one pass and it can quietly bend the steps toward the answer it already wants; force each sub-question into a sealed context and it can't.

Sealing each sub-answer in its own context removes access to the destination, so the sub-answerer has nothing to rationalize toward; the final answer is forced to be a function of independently computed pieces, not a post-hoc story.
beats.visual: A question routed into separate sealed rooms each returning one sub-answer, recomposed at the end — versus one open room where the answer leaks backward into the steps.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Q with choices A–D. Factored: room 1 → "LeBron", room 2 (no choices shown) → "Savannah James", room 3 → "Brinson" → recompose → (C), each step blind to (C).

Sealing each sub-answer in its own context removes access to the destination, so the sub-answerer has nothing to rationalize toward; the final answer is forced to be a function of independently computed pieces, not a post-hoc story.
